description: Chained LEFT JOINs make all right-side columns from every join nullable

columns.name: name
columns.note: left-side of all joins, stays non-nullable

name: chained_left_joins
query_sql:
-- @name GetUserOrderProducts
-- @returns :many
SELECT u.name, o.total, oi.quantity, p.name AS product_name, p.price FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id LEFT JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id
schema_sql: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT NOT NULL, bio TEXT, age INTEGER, score NUMERIC, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())
CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), total INTEGER NOT NULL, discount INTEGER, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())
CREATE TABLE products (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, price INTEGER NOT NULL, description TEXT, category TEXT NOT NULL)
CREATE TABLE order_items (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, order_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES orders(id), product_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES products(id), quantity INTEGER NOT NULL)

sqlc_comparison.scythe_improvement: scythe tracks nullability propagation through chained LEFT JOINs correctly
sqlc_comparison.sqlc_behavior: sqlc marks NOT NULL columns from chained joins as non-nullable, ignoring the cascading LEFT JOIN effect
